Group 2 – no visa required; passport must be valid at least 3 months (90 natural days) from intended entry date. Honduran nationals aged 18 and over must present a non-apostilled Police Clearance Certificate (Certificado de Policía Nacional) upon entry, unless they are merely transiting or hold a qualifying credential from US/Canada/Schengen/EU/UK/Iceland/Norway/Switzerland. Reinstated to Group 2 effective October 26, 2023 under bilateral agreement signed October 24, 2023.

visa_policy.notes: Costa Rica uses a four-group visa classification system per Directrices Generales de Visas de Ingreso y Permanencia para No Residentes (Alcance N° 148, La Gaceta N° 216, 17 November 2025). Group 1 (180-day visa-free, passport validity 1 day from intended entry): ~60 nationalities including USA, Canada, EU/Schengen, UK, Australia, Japan, South Korea, Israel, South Africa, Qatar, UAE, and Latin American countries. Group 2 (30-day visa-free, passport validity 3 months/90 natural days from intended entry): Pacific island states, CARICOM members not in Group 1, Central American countries El Salvador/Guatemala/Honduras/Belize, Russia, Turkey, Philippines, Malaysia, etc. Honduras reinstated to Group 2 effective October 26, 2023; Honduran nationals aged 18+ must present a non-apostilled police clearance certificate. Group 3 (consular visa required, 30-day max, USD 32, 30–60 days processing, passport validity 6 months): ~80+ countries including India, China, Colombia, Kenya, most African/Asian/Middle-Eastern nations. Group 4 (restricted visa, 30-day max extendable to 90, USD 52, up to 90 days processing, passport validity 6 months): ~23 high-risk countries. Credential exceptions for Groups 3 and 4 listed in conditional_access. No e-visa or visa-on-arrival system exists. Puerto Rico and Northern Mariana Islands were removed from visa_policy as they are US territories and their residents travel on US passports (covered under United States of America entry).

credential.subtype: multiple-entry visa in any category including refugee/asylum and type C1/D combined; excludes US-only transit visas C1, C2, and C3

conditional_access.conditions: Groups 3 and 4 nationals holding a valid US multiple-entry visa in any category - including refugee/asylum and the combined C1/D crew visa - may enter Costa Rica without a consular visa. Excluded: US visa types C1, C2, and C3 (transit-only). Minimum credential validity: 1 natural day from the intended Costa Rica entry date. Maximum stay: 30 calendar days (extendable; stay cannot exceed remaining visa validity). Source: DGME Directrices Generales de Visas, Alcance N° 148, La Gaceta N° 216, 17 November 2025 (text verified directly on migracion.go.cr/visas/).

Groups 3 and 4 nationals holding a valid US residence permit (any category including refugee and/or asylum) may enter Costa Rica without a consular visa. Minimum credential validity: 1 natural day from the intended entry date. Maximum stay: 30 calendar days (extendable; stay cannot exceed remaining validity).

credential.subtype: multiple-entry visa in any category including refugee/asylum

conditional_access.conditions: Groups 3 and 4 nationals holding a valid Canadian multiple-entry visa in any category (including refugee/asylum) may enter Costa Rica without a consular visa. Minimum credential validity: 1 natural day from the intended entry date. Maximum stay: 30 calendar days (extendable; stay cannot exceed remaining visa validity). Source: DGME Alcance N° 148 (migracion.go.cr/visas/).

Groups 3 and 4 nationals holding a valid Canadian residence permit (any category including refugee and/or asylum) may enter Costa Rica without a consular visa. Minimum credential validity: 1 natural day from the intended entry date. Maximum stay: 30 calendar days (extendable; stay cannot exceed remaining validity).

Groups 3 and 4 nationals holding a valid Schengen Category C (short-stay) visa with multiple-entry authorisation exclusively may enter Costa Rica without a consular visa. Visa must be multiple-entry; minimum validity 1 natural day from the intended entry date; visa must not have an effective start date after the intended entry date. Maximum stay: 30 calendar days (extendable; stay cannot exceed remaining visa validity). Source: DGME Alcance N° 148 (migracion.go.cr/visas/).

Groups 3 and 4 nationals holding a valid Schengen Category D (national/long-stay) visa with multiple-entry authorisation exclusively may enter Costa Rica without a consular visa. Visa must be multiple-entry; minimum validity 1 natural day from the intended entry date; visa must not have an effective start date after the intended entry date. Maximum stay: 30 calendar days (extendable; stay cannot exceed remaining visa validity).

credential.subtype: residence permit allowing multiple entries or with minimum 90 natural days validity (excluding refugee/asylum)

conditional_access.conditions: Groups 3 and 4 nationals holding a valid residence permit issued by an EU member state - that allows multiple entries OR has a minimum validity of 90 natural days from the intended entry date - may enter Costa Rica without a consular visa. Refugee and asylum categories are excluded. Document must be officially issued. Maximum stay: 30 calendar days (extendable). EU states listed on DGME page: Germany, Austria, Belgium, Bulgaria, Czech Republic, Cyprus, Croatia, Denmark, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Estonia, Finland, France, Greece, Hungary, Ireland, Italy, Latvia, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, Netherlands, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Sweden.

credential.subtype: residence permit allowing multiple entries or with minimum 90 natural days validity (excluding refugee/asylum); covers Scotland, Wales, England, Northern Ireland

conditional_access.conditions: Groups 3 and 4 nationals holding a valid UK residence permit (covering Scotland, Wales, England, or Northern Ireland) - that allows multiple entries OR has a minimum validity of 90 natural days from the intended entry date - may enter Costa Rica without a consular visa. Refugee and asylum categories are excluded. Maximum stay: 30 calendar days (extendable). Source: DGME Alcance N° 148 (migracion.go.cr/visas/).

credential.subtype: residence permit allowing multiple entries or with minimum 90 natural days validity (excluding refugee/asylum)

conditional_access.conditions: Groups 3 and 4 nationals holding a valid Icelandic residence permit - that allows multiple entries OR has a minimum validity of 90 natural days from the intended entry date - may enter Costa Rica without a consular visa. Refugee and asylum categories are excluded. Maximum stay: 30 calendar days (extendable).

credential.subtype: residence permit allowing multiple entries or with minimum 90 natural days validity (excluding refugee/asylum)

conditional_access.conditions: Groups 3 and 4 nationals holding a valid Norwegian residence permit - that allows multiple entries OR has a minimum validity of 90 natural days from the intended entry date - may enter Costa Rica without a consular visa. Refugee and asylum categories are excluded. Maximum stay: 30 calendar days (extendable).

credential.subtype: residence permit allowing multiple entries or with minimum 90 natural days validity (excluding refugee/asylum)

conditional_access.conditions: Groups 3 and 4 nationals holding a valid Swiss residence permit - that allows multiple entries OR has a minimum validity of 90 natural days from the intended entry date - may enter Costa Rica without a consular visa. Refugee and asylum categories are excluded. Maximum stay: 30 calendar days (extendable).

cbi.notes: Costa Rica does not have a citizenship-by-investment (CBI) program that grants citizenship directly in exchange for investment or donation. Citizenship requires completing a full naturalization process after extended legal residency (5 years for Central Americans, Spaniards, and Latin Americans; 7 years for all others). No official CBI program found on migracion.go.cr or rree.go.cr.

programs.name: Inversionista (Investor) Temporary Residence
programs.notes: Governed by Law No. 9996 (published in Official Gazette La Gaceta No. 135, July 14, 2021). Minimum investment of USD 150,000 in one of the recognized categories: real estate, company shares (active businesses), SUGEVAL-authorized securities, venture capital funds, tourism infrastructure, or national interest projects. Investment must be held continuously. Temporary residence issued for 2 years, renewable. Path to citizenship via naturalization: 7 years total residency (for most nationalities), or 5 years for Central Americans, Spaniards, and Latin Americans. Amount cited in Article 4 as 'not less than one hundred and fifty thousand US dollars (US$150,000.00)' - this was reduced from USD 200,000 under a sunset clause provision.

programs.name: Rentista (Fixed-Income) Temporary Residence
programs.notes: Governed by Law No. 9996 and Executive Decree No. 37112. Requires stable foreign-source monthly income of at least USD 2,500 per month. Income must come from outside Costa Rica and be demonstrated via certified bank statements. Temporary residence issued for 2 years, renewable. Path to citizenship via naturalization: 7 years (5 years for Latin Americans/Central Americans/Spaniards).

programs.name: Pensionado (Retiree) Temporary Residence
programs.notes: Governed by Ley General de Migración y Extranjería No. 8764 and Law 9996. Requires a lifetime pension of at least USD 1,000 per month from a competent authority (Social Security, military, retirement fund, annuity). Pension must be permanent. Temporary residence issued for 2 years, renewable. Path to citizenship via naturalization: 7 years (5 years for Latin Americans/Central Americans/Spaniards).

programs.name: Nómadas Digitales (Digital Nomad) – Temporary Stay Permit
programs.eligibility: Foreign nationals who provide paid remote services to a natural person or legal entity located outside Costa Rica using digital or telecommunication technologies. Must earn minimum USD 3,000/month (USD 4,000/month if including dependents) from outside Costa Rica, documented over the prior year.
programs.notes: Established under General Law of Migration and Foreigners Article 256 and Law 10008 (Item 11 bis). Duration: 1 year, renewable for a further year. Government application fee: USD 100 (or equivalent in colones, paid to Bank of Costa Rica account 001-0242480-0). Health insurance covering full stay with minimum USD 50,000 coverage required. Applications via online TramiteYa platform or in person at DGME offices. Digital nomad permit holders are exempt from paying taxes and customs duties on electronic devices for professional use.

validation_notes: CORRECTION 1 (June 2026): Group 1 passport validity changed from '1 month' to '1 natural day from intended entry date'. The DGME official page (migracion.go.cr/visas/, accessed via Firecrawl June 2026) explicitly states 'Para los nacionales ubicados en el Primer Grupo, hasta 1 día'. The prior record sourced from the Costa Rican Embassy Kenya used the more conservative '1 month' figure; the DGME primary source overrides this.

CORRECTION 2 (June 2026): Honduras added to visa_free Group 2 (30 days, police clearance for 18+ required). The prior record noted in gaps that Honduras had moved back to Group 2. This has now been confirmed from the embassy Kenya page, Fragomen alert, and search results. Honduras was reinstated effective October 26, 2023.

CORRECTION 3 (June 2026): São Tomé and Príncipe (iso3=STP) added to visa_free Group 2 (30 days). The prior record did not include this nationality; it appeared on the embassy Kenya page as 'Sao Tomé e Príncipe' in Group 2.

CORRECTION 4 (June 2026): Puerto Rico removed from visa_free. Puerto Rico is a US territory; residents travel on US passports. It is not a separate nationality for passport classification purposes. Covered under United States of America (iso3=USA). NOTE: Alcance N° 148 text does list 'Puerto Rico' in Group 1, but this is a US territorial listing - travelers hold US passports and are captured under the USA entry.

CORRECTION 5 (June 2026): Northern Mariana Islands removed from visa_free. Same rationale as Puerto Rico - US territory, residents travel on US passports. NOTE: Alcance N° 148 text lists 'Islas Marianas del Norte' in Group 2, but CNMI residents hold US passports and are captured under the USA entry.
CORRECTION 6 (June 2026): Taiwan iso3 changed from null to 'TWN'. Taiwan holds ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 code TWN (assigned as 'Taiwan, Province of China'). The prior record set this to null.

CORRECTION 7 (June 2026): US/Canada credential condition notes corrected. The DGME text (migracion.go.cr/visas/) explicitly states the combined US visa type 'C1/D' IS accepted (it is a combined crew/transit-departure visa, not a pure transit visa). Only US visa types C1, C2, and C3 issued for transit-only purposes are excluded. The prior record stated 'excluding C1/C2/C3 transit visas' which was partially accurate but did not clarify the C1/D combined visa acceptance.

CORRECTION 8 (June 2026): All source_url fields updated from 'https://costaricanembassy.co.ke/visas/' to 'https://migracion.go.cr/visas/' for entries that could be verified on the DGME primary source. Honduras and São Tomé entries retain embassy Kenya URL as those were confirmed there.
CORRECTION 9 (June 2026): visitcostarica.com removed from official_domains - it is a tourism board site and not a government immigration domain.

UNCHANGED (June 2026): RBI program amounts (USD 150,000 investor, USD 2,500 rentista, USD 1,000 pensionado) could not be re-verified directly from migracion.go.cr due to HTTP 403 on that subpage; figures retained from prior record as consistent with Law 9996 references.

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-27: Full Group 1 and Group 2 country lists re-confirmed against El Financiero's reproduction of the Alcance N° 148 text (elfinancierocr.com, accessed 2026-06-27). All 60+ Group 1 nationalities and all 36 Group 2 nationalities match the current visa_policy entries exactly (excluding Puerto Rico and Northern Mariana Islands which are US territories). Philippines (Filipinas) confirmed Group 2. Taiwan confirmed Group 2. Barbados confirmed Group 1. São Tomé (Santo Tomé y Príncipe) confirmed Group 2. Russia (Federación de Rusia) confirmed Group 2 with 30-day initial entry extendable to 90 days. No changes required to visa_policy from this re-verification run.

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-27: Schengen visa conditional_access minimum validity of '1 natural day' at entry confirmed as correct per Fragomen November 2025 resolution summary (fragomen.com/insights/costa-rica-resolution-published-adjusting-certain-visa-and-entry-rules.html). Note: Schengen visa must be (a) multiple-entry, (b) valid for at least 1 calendar day from intended entry date, (c) not have an effective start date after the intended entry date, and (d) stay in Costa Rica cannot exceed remaining visa validity. These details already correctly reflected in conditional_access entries.

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-27: Mauritania and Pakistan were reclassified from Group 3 to Group 4 (Restricted Visa) under Alcance N° 148 (November 2025), per Fragomen alert. Cuba, Jamaica, Haiti, Bangladesh, Sri Lanka, North Korea, Afghanistan, Iran, Iraq, Syria, Ethiopia, Myanmar, Eritrea, Kyrgyzstan, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan, Uzbekistan, Kazakhstan, Azerbaijan, Somalia, Mauritania, Pakistan also confirmed Group 4. This does not affect visa_policy entries (which only lists visa-free/Group 1 and Group 2 nationalities).

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-27: Honduras source_url retained as costaricanembassy.co.ke/visas/ (official embassy source). The Fragomen resolution alert and Tico Times article also independently confirm Honduras Group 2 status with non-apostilled police clearance for 18+ nationals. No change required.

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-29: Full re-verification run against official sources. (1) costaricanembassy.co.ke/visas/ re-fetched — Group 1 and Group 2 country lists match current file exactly. Embassy page lists Group 1 passport validity as '1 month' (conservative); DGME directive (Alcance N° 148) remains the authoritative source at '1 natural day from entry date', confirmed by Fragomen. (2) El Financiero reproduction of Alcance N° 148 re-fetched and cross-checked — explicitly confirms 'Ucrania' (Ukraine) in Group 1 (180 days) and 'Surinam' (Suriname) in Group 2 (30 days), resolving any potential ambiguity in embassy Kenya page output. Full Group 1 count 61 entries (excluding Puerto Rico as US territory); Group 2 count 34 entries (excluding Northern Mariana Islands as US territory). (3) Fragomen November 2025 resolution summary re-fetched — reconfirms Schengen C/D multiple-entry visa minimum validity of 1 calendar day at entry (Tico Times inaccurately described this as '90 days'; the '90 days' phrasing in the directive refers to the maximum term of a Schengen C visa, not the remaining validity required). (4) No new DGME directive or nationality group changes found for 2026 — the most recent visa policy change remains Alcance N° 148 (November 17, 2025). March 2026 DIMEX issuance changes are administrative only and do not affect entry visa_policy. (5) No changes to any visa_policy entries required — all data confirmed accurate.
gaps: Costa Rica's DGME website (migracion.go.cr/visas/) returns HTTP 403 Forbidden to direct WebFetch as of 2026-06-29; country lists verified instead via El Financiero's reproduction of the Alcance N° 148 full text and Costa Rican Embassy Kenya page.

DGME Alcance N° 148 PDF (migracion.go.cr/wp-content/.../Alcance-148-Gaceta-N°-216-17-11-2025.pdf) returns HTTP 403 Forbidden. Country lists cross-verified via secondary official and semi-official sources (El Financiero reproduction, Embassy Kenya, Fragomen).

DGME subpages (migracion.go.cr/informacion-general/, migracion.go.cr/nomadas-digitales/) return HTTP 403 Forbidden to direct WebFetch as of 2026-06-29; RBI and digital nomad details retained from prior record.
Pensionado minimum USD 1,000/month and Inversionista USD 150,000 figures retained from prior record; not re-verified directly from official DGME subpage due to HTTP 403.

Inversionista USD 150,000 sunset clause status: the regulation states this reduced amount may revert to USD 200,000 if not extended by the National Assembly; current status as of 2026-06-27 not confirmed from official page.
Honduras police clearance age threshold (18+) confirmed from Fragomen alert and embassy Kenya page but the exact article text from DGME was not fetched directly.

Dominican Republic visa status in Group 3 confirmed from embassy Kenya page; temporary maritime-entry exemption (Resolution D.JUR-0535-10-2024-ABM) exists for sea arrivals alongside Colombia and Ecuador - scope and current validity of that exemption not fully verified.
Japan and South Korea as credential issuers: not listed in DGME Alcance N° 148 text as confirmed from secondary sources; excluded from conditional_access pending any future DGME update.
OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) card: no official Costa Rican government source mentions OCI as a qualifying credential; India remains Group 3.
Diplomatic/service passport bilateral agreements: not verified from an accessible official Costa Rican government page.
Path to permanent residence (years before PR grant) for investor/rentista/pensionado not confirmed from official source; only naturalization citizenship timeline cited.

Puerto Rico and Northern Mariana Islands: Alcance N° 148 explicitly names 'Puerto Rico' in Group 1 and 'Islas Marianas del Norte' in Group 2. These are retained outside visa_policy since they are US territories (residents hold US passports), but the official text does list them - suggesting Costa Rica tracks territory of birth/registration separately. If the app ever needs to surface these specifically, they should map to USA (iso3=USA).

sources_checked.description: DGME official visa page – primary source
sources_checked.note: HTTP 403 Forbidden via direct WebFetch as of 2026-06-29 (same as 2026-06-27). Previously accessible via Firecrawl stealth proxy (June 2026). Country lists re-verified via El Financiero reproduction of Alcance N° 148 and Embassy Kenya page.

sources_checked.description: DGME official Directrices Generales de Visas – Alcance N° 148, La Gaceta N° 216, 17 November 2025
sources_checked.note: HTTP 403 Forbidden on WebFetch as of 2026-06-29

sources_checked.description: Official Costa Rican Embassy in Kenya – full visa group lists and conditional access rules
sources_checked.note: Accessed 2026-06-27 and 2026-06-29. Confirms all Group 1 and Group 2 country lists, Honduras in Group 2 with police clearance notation, São Tomé and Príncipe in Group 2, Philippines and Taiwan in Group 2, Northern Mariana Islands in Group 2. Group 1 passport validity shown as '1 month' (conservative vs. DGME directive's '1 day'). Ukraine confirmed in Group 1 via cross-check with El Financiero text. Embassy email concr-ke@rree.go.cr confirms official status.

sources_checked.description: El Financiero (Costa Rica) – full reproduction of Group 1 and Group 2 country lists from Alcance N° 148
sources_checked.note: Accessed 2026-06-27 and 2026-06-29. Reproduces the full official Group 1 list (61 nationalities excluding Puerto Rico territory) and Group 2 list (35 nationalities including Northern Mariana Islands territory) verbatim from Alcance N° 148. Explicitly confirms 'Ucrania' (Ukraine) in Group 1 and 'Surinam' (Suriname) in Group 2. Confirms Barbados in Group 1, Philippines and Taiwan in Group 2, São Tomé (Santo Tomé y Príncipe) in Group 2, Russia in Group 2, Honduras in Group 2. Puerto Rico appears in Group 1 and Northern Mariana Islands (Islas Marianas del Norte) appears in Group 2 in the official text — both excluded from visa_policy as US territories.

sources_checked.description: Fragomen – Costa Rica/Honduras visa-free entry reinstated alert
sources_checked.note: Confirms Honduras reinstated to Group 2 effective October 26, 2023; Honduran nationals aged 18+ require police clearance certificate; stay up to 30 days renewable to 90.

sources_checked.description: Fragomen – summary of DGME resolution November 2025 (Alcance N° 148)
sources_checked.note: Accessed 2026-06-27 and 2026-06-29. Confirms credential-based exemptions, Honduras reclassified to Group 2 under Alcance N° 148, Schengen visa multiple-entry requirement, Schengen visa minimum validity of 1 calendar day from intended entry date (not 90 days — the 90-day figure in directive refers to the maximum term of a Schengen C visa, not the required remaining validity), and reclassification of Mauritania and Pakistan to Group 4.

sources_checked.description: The Tico Times – Costa Rica November 2025 visa update coverage
sources_checked.note: Accessed 2026-06-27 and 2026-06-29. Confirms Philippines and Taiwan remain Group 2, Honduras stays Group 2 with police clearance for 18+, Mauritania and Pakistan moved to Group 4, credential exemptions updated. Russia stays Group 2. NOTE: Article inaccurately states Schengen visa requires '90 days of validity remaining' — this conflicts with Fragomen's direct reading of the resolution which specifies 1 calendar day. The Fragomen source is more authoritative for this detail.

sources_checked.description: Costa Rica Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Worship
sources_checked.note: Accessed 2026-06-27. Focuses on embassy/consulate listings and consular services for Costa Rican citizens. No visa entry requirement details for inbound visitors. Russia bilateral visa suppression agreement previously confirmed here.

sources_checked.description: Costa Rican Embassy Kenya – visa category listing page
sources_checked.note: Confirms four-group visa classification with entry durations, fees (Group 3: USD 32, Group 4: USD 52), processing times, transit visa, and religious provisional temporary residency.

visa_types.name: Visa-Free Entry – Group 1
visa_types.purpose: Tourism, business, family visits, and other short-stay purposes for nationals of approximately 60 privileged countries including USA, Canada, EU/Schengen, UK, Australia, Japan, South Korea, and select Latin American nations.
visa_types.notes: No visa required. Passport must be valid for at least 1 natural day from the intended entry date (per DGME Alcance N° 148, La Gaceta N° 216, 17 November 2025). Stay of up to 180 calendar days. Proof of onward travel and sufficient funds may be requested at entry.

visa_types.name: Visa-Free Entry – Group 2
visa_types.purpose: Tourism, business, and short-stay visits for nationals of Group 2 countries including most Pacific island states, certain CARICOM members, Central American countries, Russia, Turkey, Malaysia, Philippines, and others.
visa_types.notes: No visa required. Passport must be valid for at least 3 months (90 natural days) from the intended entry date. Stay of up to 30 calendar days; may be extendable to 90 days. Honduran nationals aged 18 and over must present a non-apostilled national police clearance certificate upon entry (unless they hold a qualifying US/Canada/Schengen/EU/UK/Iceland/Norway/Switzerland credential).

visa_types.name: Visa Consular – Group 3 (Consular Visa)
visa_types.purpose: General entry for nationals of countries classified in Group 3 who require a prior consular visa to enter Costa Rica for tourism, business, family visits, or other short-stay purposes.
visa_types.notes: Must be obtained in advance from a Costa Rican consulate. Passport must be valid for at least 6 months from the intended entry date. Processing time: 30–60 calendar days after receipt of all required documents. Stay of up to 30 calendar days. Group 3 covers approximately 80+ countries including India, China, Colombia, Kenya, and most African, Asian, and Middle Eastern nations. Nationals holding a valid US, Canadian, or Schengen multiple-entry visa (or qualifying residence permit) may be exempt.

visa_types.name: Visa Restringida – Group 4 (Restricted Visa)
visa_types.purpose: General entry for nationals of approximately 23 high-risk countries (including Afghanistan, Cuba, Iran, North Korea, Syria) who are subject to the most restrictive visa category.
visa_types.notes: Must be obtained in advance from a Costa Rican consulate. Passport must be valid for at least 6 months from the intended entry date. Processing time: up to 90 calendar days. Stay of up to 30 calendar days, extendable to a maximum of 90 days. Fee is USD 52. Application subject to additional security review.

visa_types.name: Visa de Tránsito (Transit Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Airport transit through Costa Rica for nationals of Group 4 and specified African and Asian countries who do not have a qualifying onward visa to an exempt destination.
visa_types.notes: Required for Group 4 nationals and certain other nationalities transiting through Costa Rican airports. Visa is issued with a 3-month (90-day) validity from the stamp date; the holder may remain in transit for up to 60 calendar days post-stamping. Stay is non-extendable. Applicant must hold an air ticket reservation and, if applicable, a valid visa for the final destination country.

visa_types.name: Residencia Provisional por Motivos Religiosos (Religious Provisional Temporary Residency)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary residency permit for foreign nationals engaged in religious work or activities through a legally registered Costa Rican religious congregation.
visa_types.notes: Classified as a 'Temporary Residency Provisional' category. Available to nationals of Group 1 through Group 3 countries (Group 4 nationals are excluded from this path). Requires a sponsoring letter from a recognized Costa Rican religious congregation. Duration is determined by the needs of the religious congregation. Application processed through the Dirección General de Migración y Extranjería (DGME).

visa_types.name: Nómadas Digitales – Permiso de Permanencia Temporal (Digital Nomad Temporary Stay Permit)
visa_types.purpose: Permits foreign nationals who work remotely for clients or employers located outside Costa Rica to reside in the country for up to one year while performing their professional activities using digital or telecommunication technologies.
visa_types.notes: Established under General Law of Migration and Foreigners Article 256 and Law 10008 (Item 11 bis). Minimum income requirement: USD 3,000/month (USD 4,000/month if including dependents), documented over the prior 12 months from sources outside Costa Rica. Government fee: USD 100 (or equivalent in colones, payable to Bank of Costa Rica account 001-0242480-0). Health insurance covering the full stay with a minimum coverage of USD 50,000 is required. Applications submitted via the online TramiteYa platform or in person at DGME offices. Duration: 1 year, renewable once for an additional year (maximum 2 years total). Permit holders are exempt from paying Costa Rican income taxes and customs duties on electronic devices used professionally.

visa_types.name: Residencia Temporal – Categoría Pensionado (Pensionado/Retiree Temporary Residency)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary residency for foreign retirees who receive a permanent lifetime pension from a recognized authority (Social Security, military, retirement fund, or annuity) of at least USD 1,000 per month.
visa_types.notes: Governed by Ley General de Migración y Extranjería No. 8764 and Law 9996. Minimum pension requirement: USD 1,000 per month from a permanent, certified foreign authority. Temporary residence is issued for 2 years and is renewable. Provides a path to citizenship via naturalization after 7 years of legal residency (5 years for Central Americans, Spaniards, and Latin Americans). Application requires notarized proof of pension, apostilled documents, criminal background check, medical certificate, and proof of accommodation. No work authorization is included; pensionados may not engage in paid employment in Costa Rica.

visa_types.name: Residencia Temporal – Categoría Inversionista (Investor Temporary Residency)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary residency for foreign nationals who make a qualifying investment of at least USD 150,000 in Costa Rica in approved categories such as real estate, company shares, SUGEVAL-authorized securities, venture capital funds, tourism infrastructure, or national interest projects.
visa_types.notes: Governed by Law No. 9996 (Official Gazette La Gaceta No. 135, July 14, 2021). Minimum qualifying investment: USD 150,000 (reduced from USD 200,000 under a sunset clause provision in Article 4). Investment must be maintained continuously. Temporary residence issued for 2 years, renewable. Permits path to citizenship after 7 years of residency (5 years for Central Americans, Spaniards, and Latin Americans). Approved investment categories include: real estate, active business shares, SUGEVAL-regulated securities, venture capital funds, tourism infrastructure, or projects declared in the national interest.

visa_types.name: Residencia Temporal – Categoría Rentista (Rentista/Fixed-Income Temporary Residency)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary residency for foreign nationals who can demonstrate stable monthly income of at least USD 2,500 per month from foreign sources, without a requirement to hold a government pension.
visa_types.notes: Governed by Law No. 9996 and Executive Decree No. 37112. Minimum income: USD 2,500 per month from stable foreign-source revenue (e.g., dividends, rental income, royalties, investment returns). Income must originate outside Costa Rica, demonstrated via certified bank statements. Temporary residence issued for 2 years, renewable. Path to citizenship via naturalization after 7 years (5 years for Central Americans, Spaniards, and Latin Americans). Rentistas may not work in Costa Rica.

outbound_official.note: no official Costa Rica government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data. The Costa Rican Ministry of Foreign Affairs (rree.go.cr) Protocolo para Viajeros Costarricenses page explicitly redirects citizens to IATA (iatatravelcentre.com) or individual embassies/consulates for outbound visa requirements - it does not publish its own enumerated list. No alternative official Costa Rican government page (migracion.go.cr, rree.go.cr, cancilleria.go.cr) with a comprehensive outbound visa-free country list for ordinary Costa Rican passport holders was located as of June 2026.
